LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PETITION WESLEY RUSCH PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 128.7. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SANCTIONS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO FUTURE MOTION UNDER DIFFERENT CIRCUMSTANCES. PLAINTIFF NEVER FILED THE SECOND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION WITH THE COURT AND THEREFORE CCP SECTION 128.7 DOES NOT APPLY AND THERE IS NOTHING TO WITHDRAW. SEE TRANS-ACTION COMM'T INVESTORS, LTD. V. TELINEK (1997) 60 CAL.APP. 4TH 352, 369. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Motion To Strike And Issue Resolution
Off Calendar Dec-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER WESLEY RUSCH'S MOTION TO STRIKE AND ISSUE RESOLUTION. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: off calendar, the case is over because the court entered judgment in defendant's favor on May 18, 2010. There is no legal basis for this motion. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Petition Wesley Rusch Pursuant To Ccp Section 128.7
Off Calendar May-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PETITION WESLEY RUSCH PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 128.7

Appearances:
Allyson E. Cook of Burnham Brown (510.444.6800) appearing for respondent and moving party
Wes Rusch (341.1699) petitioner and responding party appearing in propria persona

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: motion for sanctions denied without prejudice to future motion under different circumstances. Plaintiff never filed the second motion for reconsideration with the court and therefore CCP section 128.7 does not apply and there is nothing to withdraw. See Trans-Action Comm't Investors, Ltd. v. Telinek (1997) 60 Cal.App. 4th 352, 369. Order and judgment signed in open court. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR #5797

Motion To Strike
Off Calendar May-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER WESLEY RUSCH'S MOTION TO STRIKE

Appearances:
Allyson E. Cook of Burnham Brown (510.444.6800) appearing for respondent and opposing party
Wes Rusch (341.1699) petitioner and moving party appearing in propria persona

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike denied as untimely under CCP section 1005. No proper request for continuance before the Court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR #5797
